cross_disciplinary_pairing.description: An offline clinical-decision-support assistant for postpartum home-visit follow-up. The language model reasons over evidence-based maternal/newborn danger-sign protocols and a structured local record of each discharged mother, guiding a community health worker through both physical danger-sign checks (postpartum haemorrhage, infection, pre-eclampsia signs) and a psychosocial support assessment (isolation, postpartum depression), and producing a clear escalate-or-continue recommendation — entirely offline, for low-connectivity rural Nigerian settings.

test_prompts.prompt: A woman is 6 days postpartum. During a home visit she reports soaking more than one sanitary pad every hour and feels dizzy when she stands. What danger signs does this suggest, and what should a community health worker do?

At a home visit 10 days after delivery, a new mother seems withdrawn, is barely sleeping, and says she feels hopeless and disconnected from her baby. What should the health worker assess, and when should this be escalated to a health facility?
